input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

recent_thinks: [0-2] A large grey shark swims from right to left, its snout pointed forward and mouth slightly open, revealing teeth. The animal glides over a sandy seabed dotted with sparse, dark seagrass. The water appears hazy and blue-green. In the bottom right corner, a yellow rectangular logo and white text URL are visible.

samples.output: <think>The grey shark continues swimming steadily towards the left, gliding just above the sandy seabed scattered with dark seagrass. As it moves forward, the vertical gill slits on its side become clearly visible and prominent against its smooth skin. The yellow logo remains fixed in the bottom right corner.</think><action>silent</action>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

[6-10] The shark angles away, revealing its dorsal fin and tail as it propels into deeper blue water. Several dark remora fish are attached to its flank.
[10-14] The view cuts to a low angle looking up at the surface. Three sharks swim overhead; a large one dominates the left foreground while others swim in the distance. Their silhouettes contrast against the sunlit, rippling water as they move away.

[14-18] A grey shark with prominent gill slits swims over a sandy bottom with scattered seagrass, a dark remora attached to its upper back. A second, darker shark body appears in the lower foreground, swimming closely beneath the initial shark; multiple remoras, including a long silver one, cling to their skins.
[18-22] A large shark body dominates the left foreground while a second shark swims away toward the right background. Then, a large grey shark swims horizontally left to right through bright blue water carrying attached remoras, while another shark swims parallel near the sunlit surface above.
[22-26] The camera angles upward from the seabed, capturing a large shark's dark silhouette passing overhead against the light, with another shark distant to the left. A close-up from below reveals the shark's underside, mouth slightly open to display rows of sharp, triangular teeth.
[26-28] The shark's pale underbelly fills the frame as it swims directly overhead above the sandy seabed. The text "natgeotv.com" is visible in the bottom right corner.

[28-32] An extreme close-up reveals a shark's grey head and dark eye swimming right, followed by a blurry transition to its pale underbelly against blue water. [32-34] A low-angle wide shot shows a large school of sharks silhouetted against the sunlit surface, circling overhead. [34-38] Two large grey sharks swim horizontally right with dark remora fish attached; the camera tracks one closely, showing a remora on its dorsal fin near surface ripples. [38-42] Close-ups feature a shark's eye, snout pores, and broad head over a white sandy seabed, with a remora swimming independently nearby. [42-44] The view returns to a low angle, capturing multiple sharks swimming overhead in various directions against the bright blue water. The yellow National Geographic logo and "natgeotv.com" text persist in the bottom right corner.

recent_thinks: [44-46] The camera angle drops to the seabed, showing a large grey shark swimming right over white sand and sparse seagrass. Dark remora fish cling to its back near the dorsal fin. Another shark's tail and rear body are visible in the upper right background. The yellow logo remains in the corner.

[46-48] A close-up shows a grey shark's head and open mouth with visible teeth swimming over seagrass. Another shark's tail is in the background. The view transitions to an extreme close-up of the shark's pale underbelly passing directly over the camera near the sandy bottom.

[48-50] A dark shark tail fin swims away to the right, moving over a white sandy seabed scattered with sparse seagrass. The clear blue water fills the background, and the yellow logo remains visible in the bottom right corner as the shark's rear body exits the frame, leaving the empty seabed visible.

[50-52] A large grey shark swims horizontally from left to right through clear blue water, occupying the center of the frame. A second shark is visible swimming parallel in the background, positioned slightly higher. The scene shifts from the sandy seabed to open water, highlighting the sharks' profiles against the blue ocean.

[52-54] A large grey shark swims rightward through clear blue water, displaying dark remora fish attached to its back and flank. Another shark swims below it in the same direction. Sunlight filters through the surface ripples above. The yellow logo and website text persist in the bottom right corner.

samples.output: <think>A large grey shark with remoras swims towards the left foreground, facing the camera with its mouth slightly open. In the background, other sharks swim towards the right. The water is bright blue with surface ripples visible above. The yellow logo is present.</think><action>silent</action>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

[6-10] The shark angles away, revealing its dorsal fin and tail as it propels into deeper blue water. Several dark remora fish are attached to its flank.
[10-14] The view cuts to a low angle looking up at the surface. Three sharks swim overhead; a large one dominates the left foreground while others swim in the distance. Their silhouettes contrast against the sunlit, rippling water as they move away.

[14-18] A grey shark with prominent gill slits swims over a sandy bottom with scattered seagrass, a dark remora attached to its upper back. A second, darker shark body appears in the lower foreground, swimming closely beneath the initial shark; multiple remoras, including a long silver one, cling to their skins.
[18-22] A large shark body dominates the left foreground while a second shark swims away toward the right background. Then, a large grey shark swims horizontally left to right through bright blue water carrying attached remoras, while another shark swims parallel near the sunlit surface above.
[22-26] The camera angles upward from the seabed, capturing a large shark's dark silhouette passing overhead against the light, with another shark distant to the left. A close-up from below reveals the shark's underside, mouth slightly open to display rows of sharp, triangular teeth.
[26-28] The shark's pale underbelly fills the frame as it swims directly overhead above the sandy seabed. The text "natgeotv.com" is visible in the bottom right corner.

[28-32] An extreme close-up reveals a shark's grey head and dark eye swimming right, followed by a blurry transition to its pale underbelly against blue water. [32-34] A low-angle wide shot shows a large school of sharks silhouetted against the sunlit surface, circling overhead. [34-38] Two large grey sharks swim horizontally right with dark remora fish attached; the camera tracks one closely, showing a remora on its dorsal fin near surface ripples. [38-42] Close-ups feature a shark's eye, snout pores, and broad head over a white sandy seabed, with a remora swimming independently nearby. [42-44] The view returns to a low angle, capturing multiple sharks swimming overhead in various directions against the bright blue water. The yellow National Geographic logo and "natgeotv.com" text persist in the bottom right corner.

[46-48] A close-up shows a grey shark's head and teeth over seagrass, then its pale underbelly passes over the camera near the sandy bottom. [50-54] In open water, a large grey shark swims left to right with dark remora fish attached to its back, accompanied by a second shark swimming parallel below under sunlight filtering through the surface. [54-56] A grey shark with remoras swims toward the left foreground facing the camera, mouth slightly open, while background sharks swim right. [56-58] The view looks upward at a shark's pale underbelly passing overhead while another swims right below; finally, multiple sharks navigate near the surface, with one prominent in the lower foreground, as "natgeotv.com" appears.

[58-62] A large shark with faint vertical stripes swims right over a white sandy seabed with sparse seagrass, while a school of sharks navigates below. A close-up reveals two remora fish clinging to the shark's tail section. [62-66] A low-angle shot captures a shark's pale underbelly and tail swimming overhead against the bright surface, with another shark silhouetted in the distance.
